title: ferray-autodiff: no jacobian() function for vector-valued functions
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

gradient() computes the gradient of a scalar-valued multivariate function. But there's no jacobian() for vector-valued functions f: R^n -> R^m. The Jacobian matrix J[i][j] = df_i/dx_j is needed for: (1) neural network layer derivatives, (2) optimization with vector outputs, (3) sensitivity analysis. Forward-mode computes one column of J per evaluation, so jacobian(f, point) would call f n times (same as gradient but collecting all m output duals per call).
